title: How To Pass Maverick Trading Challenge: Step-By-Step Guide (2026)

content:
Table of Contents

  • Maverick Trading Challenge Overview (2026)
  • Step-by-Step: How to Pass Maverick Trading Challenge
  • Costs, Payouts, and Key Features
  • Quick Comparison: Maverick Trading vs. Competitors
  • Top Tips & Strategies for 2026
  • Common Mistakes to Avoid
  • Final Recommendation: Is Maverick Trading Right for You?

Maverick Trading Challenge Overview (2026)

Maverick Trading stands out in the prop firm world for its training-first approach, making it a top choice for traders who want to build real skills—not just pass a one-off challenge. As of 2026, Maverick Trading continues to offer a unique pathway to funded trading, focusing on US equities and options. Unlike many challenge-based firms, Maverick requires a higher upfront commitment ($2,500/month), but provides structured mentorship, a 25+ year track record, and up to 80% profit split for successful traders.

For a detailed breakdown of their offering, see our Maverick Trading review.

Step-by-Step: How to Pass Maverick Trading Challenge

Passing the Maverick Trading Challenge is less about beating a simulated profit target and more about demonstrating consistent, disciplined trading within their risk parameters. Here’s a step-by-step guide to help you navigate the process in 2026:

  1. Apply and Complete the Interview: Submit your application and complete a video interview. Maverick screens for commitment, risk awareness, and willingness to learn.
  2. Enroll in Training: Pay the program fee ($2,500/month as of 2026) and begin their comprehensive training modules. This covers options strategies, risk management, and trading psychology.
  3. Pass the Simulated Trading Phase: Trade in a simulated environment, following strict risk controls. You must demonstrate consistent profitability and adherence to Maverick’s rules—no reckless bets or outsized drawdowns.
  4. Risk Review and Evaluation: Your trades are reviewed by Maverick’s risk team. They look for discipline, not just profits. Expect feedback and possibly additional simulation if you deviate from guidelines.
  5. Transition to Live Capital: Once approved, you’ll receive a funded account (size varies based on your performance and risk profile). You’ll continue to receive mentorship and performance reviews.

This process is designed to filter for traders who can thrive long-term, not just pass a short-term challenge.

Costs, Payouts, and Key Features

Understanding the financial commitment and potential rewards is crucial before you start. Here’s what you need to know about Maverick Trading in 2026:

  • Program Cost: $2,500/month (higher than most challenge-based firms, but includes training and mentorship)
  • Profit Split: Up to 80% to the trader
  • Markets: US equities and options only (no forex, futures, or crypto)
  • Track Record: 25+ years in business
  • Rating: 3.9/5 (see our Maverick Trading review for details)
  • Best For: Beginners seeking structured mentorship, options traders, those prioritizing education
  • Cons: Higher upfront cost, limited asset classes

For a side-by-side look at how Maverick stacks up against other top prop firms, check out our comparison pages.

Quick Comparison: Maverick Trading vs. Competitors

Prop Firm Rating Monthly Cost Profit Split Markets Best For
Maverick Trading 3.9/5 $2,500 Up to 80% US equities, options Beginners, options traders, mentorship
Apex Trader Funding 4.5/5 $147 Up to 90% Futures Futures traders, low-cost challenge
AquaFunded 4.2/5 $199 Up to 85% Forex, indices, crypto Forex/crypto traders, fast funding
Blue Guardian 4.0/5 $249 Up to 85% Forex, indices Forex traders, challenge-based

For more details, see our AquaFunded vs. Maverick Trading and Blue Guardian vs. Maverick Trading comparison pages.

Top Tips & Strategies for Passing Maverick Trading in 2026

  • Embrace the Training: Maverick’s edge is its education. Complete every module, attend live sessions, and ask questions.
  • Follow the Rules: Maverick is strict about risk management. Never exceed position size or daily loss limits.
  • Document Your Trades: Keep a detailed trading journal. This helps during risk reviews and shows your commitment to improvement.
  • Focus on Consistency: Maverick values steady, repeatable results over big wins. Aim for small, consistent profits.
  • Leverage Mentorship: Use your access to experienced traders. Their feedback can be the difference between passing and failing.

Common Mistakes to Avoid

  • Ignoring Risk Controls: Even one violation can set you back or disqualify you.
  • Chasing Losses: Maverick’s evaluators look for emotional discipline. Revenge trading is a red flag.
  • Skipping Training: The program is designed to build habits. Skipping steps can leave gaps in your knowledge.
  • Overtrading: Quality over quantity. Stick to your plan and avoid unnecessary trades.

Final Recommendation: Is Maverick Trading Right for You?

If you’re a beginner or options trader seeking a long-term trading career with structured mentorship, Maverick Trading is a strong choice in 2026—despite its higher upfront cost. The firm’s training-first model, 25+ year track record, and up to 80% profit split make it ideal for those who value education and discipline over quick funding. However, if you want lower costs, faster funding, or access to forex/crypto, consider alternatives like Apex Trader Funding or AquaFunded. For a side-by-side breakdown, visit our comparison page.

Bottom line: Maverick Trading isn’t for everyone, but if you’re serious about building a sustainable trading career in US equities and options, and you’re willing to invest in your education, it’s one of the best prop firms to consider in 2026.
